aboutGame.title: What is Dead or Alive 6 Last Round?
paragraphs: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round is the current-generation definitive edition of Team NINJA's fast-paced 3D fighting game. Its combat revolves around a tactical triangle of strikes, throws, and holds, supported by explosive stage hazards and cinematic Break Gauge attacks.

The Last Round edition launches with 29 playable fighters, five new costumes, current-generation optimization, and a flexible Photo Mode. A free Core Fighters edition opens most modes with four starting characters, while the full edition includes the main roster and Story unlock.

explore.title: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Guide Modules
explore.description: Jump straight to character lists, beginner systems, Core Fighters details, Photo Mode steps, save transfer notes, DLC rules, and stage hazard guides.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Characters
modules.description: Explore the complete playable roster and see which fighters are included in the full version. The page separates the original 24-character lineup, the five returning bonus fighters, and the separately sold collaboration characters.

highlights.label: Full Roster
highlights.detail: The standard version includes 29 playable characters.

highlights.label: Original Fighters
highlights.detail: Kasumi, Marie Rose, Honoka, NiCO, Helena, Hayate, Hayabusa, Jann Lee, Zack, Diego, Rig, Hitomi, Leifang, Ayane, Bayman, Bass, Tina, Mila, Christie, Kokoro, La Mariposa, Brad Wong, Eliot, and Raidou.

highlights.label: Bonus Fighters
highlights.detail: Nyotengu, Phase 4, Tamaki, Momiji, and Rachel are added to the original 24-character lineup.

highlights.label: Collab Fighters
highlights.detail: Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond are listed separately and require their own Character Unlock Keys.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Beginner Guide
modules.description: Learn the core systems that make Dead or Alive different from other 3D fighting games. This guide covers the triangle system, Fatal Rush, the Break Gauge, defensive Holds, and stage hazards in a practical learning order.

highlights.detail: Start with Tutorial and Free Training to learn movement, guarding, strikes, throws, and Holds.
Memorize the triangle system: strikes beat throws, throws beat Holds, and Holds beat strikes.
Practice Fatal Rush, a Special-button combo of up to four attacks designed to give new players an accessible offensive sequence.
Build the Break Gauge through combat; spend 50% on Break Hold or 100% on Break Blow.
Use walls, crowds, breakable objects, and Danger Zones to extend damage and create advantageous situations.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Core Fighters
modules.description: Compare the free-to-play Core Fighters edition with the paid standard version before downloading. The page explains the free roster, included modes, unlock requirements, and which features remain available without purchasing the full game.

highlights.label: Free Fighters
highlights.detail: Kasumi, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO are playable from the start.

highlights.label: Modes
highlights.detail: Core Fighters includes Versus, Arcade, Time Attack, Survival, training modes, Ranked Match, Lobby Match, DOA Central, and Photo Mode.

highlights.label: Story
highlights.detail: Story Mode is excluded unless a Story Unlock Key is purchased.

highlights.label: Unlock Keys
highlights.detail: Characters outside the four-fighter starting roster require separate Character Unlock Keys in Core Fighters.

highlights.label: Full Version
highlights.detail: The paid standard version includes the Story Unlock Key and Character Unlock Keys for its 29-character roster.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Modes and Crossplay
modules.description: Check every major offline, training, customization, and online mode included in Last Round. The page also clarifies the game's cross-platform limitations so players know who they can match with.

highlights.label: Offline
highlights.detail: Versus, Arcade, Time Attack, and Survival modes are available.

highlights.label: Training
highlights.detail: Free Training, Tutorial, Command Training, and Combo Training are included.

highlights.label: Progression
highlights.detail: Story Mode and DOA Quest provide structured single-player content.

highlights.label: Online
highlights.detail: Ranked Match and Lobby Match are supported.

highlights.label: DOA Central
highlights.detail: Wardrobe, Database, Theater, Music, Library, and Photo Mode are available from the central hub.

highlights.label: Crossplay
highlights.detail: Crossplay between different platforms is not supported, and Dead or Alive 6 cannot play online with Dead or Alive 6 Last Round.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Photo Mode Guide
modules.description: Create character portraits and action scenes with the new Photo Mode. This guide explains the official workflow for choosing a scene, positioning fighters, changing poses and expressions, and capturing the finished image.

highlights.detail: Open Photo Mode and choose the stage and characters for the scene.
Move, rotate, and place each character to build the composition.
Select character skills or poses to create an action shot or a staged portrait.
Adjust facial expressions, sweat, dirt, and other visual details.
Capture the final image with the screenshot function provided by the selected platform.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Save Transfer Guide
modules.description: Move eligible Dead or Alive 6 progression, owned items, and costumes into Last Round without losing supported data. The instructions cover the shared-account requirement, platform-specific preparation, the in-game transfer prompt, and important restrictions.

highlights.detail: Use the same PlayStation Network, Xbox, or Steam account that owns the original save data and eligible DLC.
On PlayStation, update the PlayStation 4 version of Dead or Alive 6 to version 1.23, open the game, and upload the DLC data from the Main Menu.
On Xbox Series X|S or Steam, start Last Round with the same account so eligible linked DLC can be detected automatically.
Make the original save accessible on the new console or PC, launch Last Round, and select Transfer when the title-screen prompt appears.
Complete the import before creating Last Round save data; an existing Last Round save must be deleted before the old save can be transferred.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Costumes and DLC
modules.description: See what new cosmetic content is included and which purchases from Dead or Alive 6 can carry over. The page also identifies important exceptions involving collaboration costumes, character unlocks, and Premium Tickets.

highlights.label: New Costumes
highlights.detail: Last Round adds five new costumes for Kasumi, Ayane, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO.

highlights.label: Old Costumes
highlights.detail: Most previously purchased Dead or Alive 6 costumes can transfer when the same platform account is used.

highlights.label: Collab Costumes
highlights.detail: Previously purchased costumes for Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond cannot be carried over.

highlights.label: Unlocks
highlights.detail: Additional-character unlocks from Dead or Alive 6 do not transfer; the standard edition includes 29 fighters, while Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond require separate keys.

highlights.label: Tickets
highlights.detail: Premium Tickets from the PlayStation 4 version of Dead or Alive 6 cannot be used in Last Round.

modules.name: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Stages and Danger Zones
modules.description: Learn the environmental hazards, transitions, and destructible features found across Last Round's arenas. The stage guide highlights locations where fireworks, crowds, walls, vehicles, ropes, and breakable objects can change a match.

highlights.label: Oboro
highlights.detail: Lost Paradise: Oboro is an enhanced version of the tropical stage updated with the new Oboro lighting system.

highlights.label: Festival
highlights.detail: Unused fireworks scattered around the Chinese Festival stage can explode when disturbed.

highlights.label: Unforgettable
highlights.detail: A gallery-like arena combining memorable locations and functioning Danger Zones from earlier Dead or Alive games.

highlights.label: Hidden Garden
highlights.detail: Knocking an opponent through the bamboo-covered hillside transitions the fight into the secluded Hidden Garden.

highlights.label: The Muscle
highlights.detail: A wrestling arena featuring fire pillars and electrically charged ring ropes.

highlights.label: Colosseum
highlights.detail: The tournament's main martial-arts stage triggers explosions when fighters touch the ring ropes.

highlights.label: Rumble Danger
highlights.detail: Spectators can push a launched fighter back into the arena and leave them staggered.

highlights.label: Destruction
highlights.detail: Boxes, jars, and other stage objects can be broken for extra damage and do not regenerate.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Quick answers to the most common questions about Dead or Alive 6 Last Round.

items.question: What is Dead or Alive 6 Last Round?
items.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round is the current-generation definitive edition of Team NINJA's 3D fighting game for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam. It focuses on the strike, throw, and hold triangle, stage hazards, Break Gauge attacks, and Photo Mode.

items.question: How many fighters are in Dead or Alive 6 Last Round?
items.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round includes 29 playable fighters in the standard version, with the original roster expanded by Nyotengu, Phase 4, Tamaki, Momiji, and Rachel. Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond are handled as separate collaboration fighter unlocks.

items.question: What is included in Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Core Fighters?
items.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Core Fighters starts with Kasumi, Marie Rose, Honoka, and NiCO, plus modes such as Versus, Arcade, Time Attack, Survival, training, Ranked Match, Lobby Match, DOA Central, and Photo Mode. Story Mode and additional fighters require unlock keys.

items.question: Does Dead or Alive 6 Last Round support crossplay?
items.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round does not support crossplay between different platforms. Online play is also separated from the original Dead or Alive 6, so the two versions cannot match with each other online.

items.question: Can Dead or Alive 6 saves and DLC transfer to Dead or Alive 6 Last Round?
items.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round can import eligible progress, owned items, and supported costumes when the same platform account is used. Some content has exceptions, including collaboration costumes, additional-character unlocks, and PlayStation 4 Premium Tickets.

items.question: What can players do in Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Photo Mode?
items.answer: Dead or Alive 6 Last Round Photo Mode lets players choose stages and fighters, move and rotate characters, set poses or skills, adjust expressions and visual details, and capture the scene with the selected platform screenshot function.

finalCta.title: Ready to Master Dead or Alive 6 Last Round?
finalCta.description: From your first counter hold to advanced wall pressure, character matchups, Photo Mode setups, and save-transfer notes, this community wiki helps you spend less time searching and more time fighting.
